summary: Advanced the Bob-omb/BombHei item decompilation by replacing many placeholder stubs in src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.c with C implementations, adding/refining BombHei item variable and attribute types, and simplifying helper inlines for preserving bone transform state across item animation changes. The PR also renamed the global item parameter field x30 to x30_lifetime at call sites and made small formatting/cleanup edits in related item, fighter, and stage files. The author noted several remaining itbombhei functions were still only around 90-96% by easy_funcs, so the PR was an incremental improvement rather than a fully matched completion.
what_changed: Implemented many previously placeholder-marked BombHei routines, including it_3F14_Logic6_Dropped, it_8027E978, itBombhei_UnkMotion3_Anim, it_8027EE04, itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Anim, itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Coll, it_8027F42C, itBombhei_UnkMotion4_Anim, it_8027F8E0, fn_8027FCA8, fn_8028007C, it_3F14_Logic6_Thrown, itBombhei_UnkMotion10_Anim, fn_80280974, it_80280B60, and it_3F14_Logic6_EnteredAir.

Changed BombHei state-transition helper usage so itBombhei_UpdateStatePreserveBone takes an explicit animation flags argument instead of hardcoding ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, and replaced several numbered helper variants with a single fake setter variant, itBombhei_UpdateStatePreserveBoneFake.
Added a local GET_ATTRS(ip) macro in itbombhei.c to cast ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes to itBombHeiAttributes*.
Refined itBombHei_ItemVars and itBombHeiAttributes in itCommonItems.h: xDF4 changed from s32 to float, _E04 became f32 xE04, attribute xC changed from s32 to float, and a Vec3 x20 field was added.
Updated item lifetime/global parameter field references from it_804D6D28->x30 to it_804D6D28->x30_lifetime in item.c, it_2725.c, itfreeze.c, itgshell.c, itmatadogas.c, and itbombhei.c.
Adjusted BombHei spawn logic to compute facing_dir from data.prev_pos instead of data.pos.
Added includes used by the new BombHei code, including math.h, it/forward.h, and lb/lb_00F9.h.
Made mostly formatting-only cleanup in itlikelike.c, grinishie2.c, it_27CF.c, and ft_0C31 prototypes.
smart_moves: Generalized the preserve-bone animation helper by passing flags explicitly; this removed duplicated helpers that differed only in ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE versus ITEM_UNK_0x1 and made call sites show the required animation mode.
Kept a separate fake setter helper when matching required fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslateY/fake_HSD_JObjSetRotationX, preserving a matching tactic without duplicating every flag-specific variant.
Captured and restored BombHei dynamic bone 0xB translation/rotation around Item_80268E5C state changes, a recurring pattern needed by multiple motion transitions.
Used attribute and item-var type corrections to enable float math in BombHei behavior, including velocity, timers, rotation increments, and Vec3 bounds checks.
Documented progress with easy_funcs output, making clear which itbombhei functions were still below 100% and where future matching work should focus.
decomp_lessons: When an item state transition needs to preserve a dynamic bone pose, read the JObj translation/rotation before Item_80268E5C and restore afterward; if matching requires fake setters, isolate that in a helper rather than spreading fake calls everywhere.
Do not assume unknown article attribute fields are integers; matching BombHei required changing xC and item-var xDF4 to float and adding a Vec3 attribute at x20.
Field renames such as x30_lifetime should be propagated across common item call sites to clarify meaning and reduce future confusion.

Local attribute macros can improve readability for repeated specialAttributes casts, but reviewers questioned whether inconsistent macro use is worth it if permuter/matching forces some call sites to break the pattern.
An incremental PR can still be valuable even when some functions remain 90-96% matched, as long as it removes stubs and exposes concrete future targets.
naming_conventions: Bob-omb item symbols use the project spelling/capitalization BombHei in types and functions: itBombHeiAttributes, itBombHei_ItemVars, itBombhei_UnkMotion*_Anim/Phys/Coll.
Known item variable fields retain offset-style names when semantics remain unknown, e.g. xDD4, xDE0, xDF8, xDFC, xE0C.
Semantic global item parameter naming improved from x30 to x30_lifetime.
The helper name itBombhei_UpdateStatePreserveBoneFake indicates the use of fake JObj setter calls for matching rather than a semantic gameplay difference.
Unmatched or unclear functions retained address-like names such as fn_8028007C and fn_80280974 rather than inventing behavior names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK annotations in several BombHei implementations to shape stack layout.
Used fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslateY and fake_HSD_JObjSetRotationX in itBombhei_UpdateStatePreserveBoneFake where regular setters did not produce the desired match.
Used explicit casts around Item_80268E5C calls, e.g. (HSD_GObj*) igp/arg0, matching existing item code patterns.
Sometimes avoided or broke out the GET_ATTRS macro for permuter reasons; the author specifically noted breaking out article data in some cases to reach 100%.
Used ABS comparisons against itBombHeiAttributes.x20 Vec3 components in fn_8028007C and fn_80280974 before routing to it_80280DC0.

Author-reported easy_funcs scores showed remaining hard targets: fn_80280974 at 91.04%, itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Coll at 94.14%, itBombhei_UnkMotion3_Anim at 96.15%, itBombhei_UnkMotion10_Anim at 90.32%, itBombhei_UnkMotion8_Anim at 90.36%, itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Anim at 94.64%, itBombhei_UnkMotion4_Anim at 95.26%, and fn_8028007C at 92.53%.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Questioned whether the GET_ATTRS(ip) macro meaningfully fixed stack/matching issues and noted that fighter code has similar patterns without defining macros. Mentioned a possible generic form such as GET_ATTRS(ip, itBombHeiAttributes), but said it had not been implemented.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.comment: Explained the macro was copied from itlikelike and made code more readable, especially when avoiding long casts like ((itBombHeiAttributes*) ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes)->field, but also admitted it sometimes had to be broken for permuter reasons.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Suggested that if the macro pattern cannot be used consistently it should probably be avoided, though using it or not did not matter much pending further investigation.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for itbombhei GET_ATTRS to see whether the local macro was kept, removed, or replaced by a generic GET_ATTRS(ip, type) pattern.
Search for fn_80280974 and fn_8028007C to see whether later decomp work named or fully matched these BombHei routines.
Search for x30_lifetime to confirm all former it_804D6D28->x30 uses were renamed consistently.
Search for itBombhei_UpdateStatePreserveBoneFake to determine whether fake setter helpers became accepted practice or were later eliminated.
Compare later easy_funcs output for src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.c against the 90-96% functions listed in this PR.
